questions.question_text: You are going to turn right at the intersection. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?

answers.1: I remain in my lane up to the pedestrian crossing.
answers.2: I move into the bus lane and stay there up to the pedestrian crossing.
answers.3: I cross the bus lane and pull in close to the kerb before the cycle path.

questions.question_text: You are driving at the maximum permitted speed. What should you pay particular attention to here?

answers.2: That the expressway is ending.
answers.3: That cars are prohibited here.
answers.4: That I may continue at 80 km/h after the sign.

questions.question_text: You are going to turn right here. There are no cyclists behind you on the cycle path. What do you do?

answers.2: I flash my headlights.
answers.3: I wait for the oncoming cyclist to pass.
answers.4: I pay particular attention to the fact that I am crossing a two-lane cycle path.

questions.question_text: You are travelling at about 50 km/h, the same speed as the driver ahead. What do you do?

answers.2: I increase my distance from the vehicle ahead.
answers.3: I pay particular attention to the condition of the carriageway.

questions.question_text: What should you pay particular attention to here?

answers.1: The risk of animals on the road.
answers.2: That overtaking is prohibited.
answers.3: A rider ahead on the road.

questions.question_text: You are going to turn left at the intersection. What do you do?

answers.1: I pay particular attention to oncoming traffic.
answers.2: I move forward to the centre of the intersection.
answers.3: I turn left immediately.

questions.question_text: Which lights must you use here?

answers.2: I must use parking lights and indicators.
answers.3: I must use dipped headlights or main beam.
answers.4: Automatic daytime running lights are sufficient.

questions.question_text: You are stationary at the roadside and are about to move off. What do you do?

answers.2: I pay particular attention to oncoming traffic.
answers.3: I turn my head and check whether the road is clear behind me.
answers.4: I wait a moment before moving off.

questions.question_text: You must continue straight ahead. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?

answers.1: I drive around the lorry when the oncoming traffic has passed.
answers.2: I wait until the lorry has finished unloading and driven away.
answers.3: I choose another route.

questions.question_text: You are travelling at about 50 km/h. What do you do?

answers.2: I pay particular attention to reduced grip ahead.
answers.3: I place my foot over the brake.

questions.question_text: You have caught up with two slowly moving caravans. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?

answers.2: I overtake only the nearest caravan and pull in ahead of it.
answers.3: I reduce speed and stay behind.
answers.4: I keep as far to the right as possible.

questions.question_text: You have caught up with a slowly moving car. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?

answers.2: I move my foot over to the brake.
answers.3: I pay particular attention to the course of the road.

questions.question_text: You are stationary and want to move off from the roadside. What do you do?

answers.2: I pay particular attention to the black car that has just passed.
answers.3: I turn my head and check what is happening behind me.
answers.4: I remain stationary a little longer.

questions.question_text: You are driving at 50 km/h in a built-up area and are about to overtake the slowly moving grey car. What do you do?

answers.1: I increase speed and overtake the car.
answers.2: I continue at 50 km/h and overtake the car.
answers.3: I reduce speed and abandon the overtaking manoeuvre.
answers.4: I pull in behind the grey car.

questions.question_text: You are driving at about 70 km/h. What do you do?

answers.2: I keep close to the edge line on the right.
answers.3: I drive over the edge line on the right.

questions.question_text: You are driving at about 40 km/h. What do you do?

answers.2: I increase speed.
answers.3: I stop before the pedestrian crossing.

questions.question_text: You are driving at about 40 km/h. What do you do?

answers.2: I flash my headlights.
answers.3: I keep as far to the right in the lane as possible.

questions.question_text: You are driving at about 40 km/h and must continue straight ahead. What do you do?

answers.2: I increase speed.
answers.3: I brake hard.

questions.question_text: You are driving at about 50 km/h. What do you do?

answers.2: I reduce speed.
answers.3: I move further to the right in the lane.

questions.question_text: You are driving on a one-way road and are catching up with a slowly moving truck. What do you do?

answers.1: I pass the truck on the right.
answers.2: I remain behind the truck.
answers.3: I pay particular attention to the course of the road.
answers.4: I prepare to move into the left traffic lane.

questions.question_text: You want to pass the slow-moving tractor, but there has been oncoming traffic the whole time. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?

answers.1: I pay particular attention to oncoming traffic.
answers.2: I overtake if there is nothing behind me.
answers.3: I stay behind the tractor.

questions.question_text: What does the police officer's hand signal mean?

answers.1: I may drive straight through the intersection.
answers.2: I must stop.
answers.3: I may turn left in the intersection.
answers.4: I may turn right in the intersection.

questions.question_text: What should you pay particular attention to here?

answers.2: The surroundings of the road.
answers.3: A slippery road surface ahead.

questions.question_text: You have got out of the red car. Which rule applies here?

answers.2: I may leave the car here for 2-3 minutes.

questions.question_text: You have got out of the red car. Which rule applies here?

answers.2: I may leave the car here for 2-3 minutes.
answers.3: Stopping and parking are prohibited here.
